info.title: Lifx.Cli
info.description: Command-line tool for controlling LIFX smart lights via Cloud API or LAN protocol. Features secure credential storage, comprehensive commands for lights/effects/scenes, and works with or without internet connection.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription:
LIFX CLI - Control your LIFX smart lights from the command line

Features:
  - Cloud API: Control lights via internet (requires API token)
  - LAN Protocol: Control lights on local network (no API token needed)
  - Secure credential storage via Windows Credential Manager

Quick Start (Cloud):
  1. Get API token: https://cloud.lifx.com/settings
  2. Store token: lifx cloud key set <token>
  3. Control lights: lifx cloud lights on all

Quick Start (LAN - no token needed):
  lifx lan discover

Examples:
  lifx cloud key set <token>         # Store API token (do this first!)
  lifx cloud lights list             # List all lights
  lifx cloud lights on all           # Turn on all lights
  lifx cloud lights color all blue   # Set all lights to blue
  lifx cloud effects breathe all     # Start breathe effect
  lifx cloud scenes list             # List available scenes
  lifx lan discover                  # Discover local devices

Get help for any command:
  lifx cloud --help
  lifx lan --help
  lifx cloud lights --help

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose output with detailed information [default: False]

options.name: --token
options.description: LIFX Cloud API token (overrides stored credential)

commands.name: cloud
commands.description:
Control LIFX lights via Cloud API (requires API token)

The Cloud API allows you to control your LIFX lights from anywhere with internet.
Requires an API token from https://cloud.lifx.com/settings

Subcommands:
  key       - Manage API token (set/show/delete)
  lights    - Control light power, color, and brightness
  effects   - Run visual effects (breathe, pulse, etc.)
  scenes    - List and activate scenes

Examples:
  lifx cloud key set <token>         # Store API token (REQUIRED)
  lifx cloud lights list             # List all lights
  lifx cloud lights on all           # Turn on all lights
  lifx cloud lights color all blue   # Set color
  lifx cloud effects breathe all     # Start breathe effect
  lifx cloud scenes activate "Movie Time"  # Activate scene

Setup:
  1. Get token: https://cloud.lifx.com/settings
  2. Store token: lifx cloud key set <token>
  3. Run commands: lifx cloud lights on all

commands.name: key
commands.description:
Manage LIFX Cloud API token (securely stored)

The API token is required for all Cloud API features.
Storage method depends on your platform:
  - Windows: Encrypted using Data Protection API (DPAPI)
  - Linux/macOS: Stored in ~/.lifx/credentials.json with 600 permissions

Get your API token:
  1. Visit https://cloud.lifx.com/settings
  2. Log in to your LIFX account
  3. Click 'Generate New Token'
  4. Copy the token and store it using: lifx cloud key set <token>

Security Note:
  - Never share your API token
  - Token is encrypted (Windows) or protected by file permissions (Unix)
  - Clear your command history after setting token

commands.name: set
commands.description: Store API token securely using Windows DPAPI

commands.name: show
commands.description: Check if API token is configured (does not display token)

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all lights, groups, or locations

options.name: --type
options.description: Type to list (lights, groups, locations) [default: lights]

arguments.name: selector
arguments.description: Which lights to turn on [default: all]

arguments.name: selector
arguments.description: Which lights to turn off [default: all]

arguments.name: color
arguments.description: Color (name, rgb:R,G,B, or hue:H saturation:S brightness:B)

commands.name: lan
commands.description:
Control LIFX lights via LAN protocol (no API token needed)

The LAN protocol allows you to control lights on your local network.
Works without internet connection and without API token.

Subcommands:
  discover  - Discover LIFX devices on local network
  list      - List cached discovered devices
  lights    - Control discovered lights (on/off/color)

Examples:
  lifx lan discover --timeout 10    # Discover for 10 seconds
  lifx lan list                     # Show discovered devices
  lifx lan lights on D0:73:D5:XX:XX:XX   # Turn on a light
  lifx lan lights color D0:73:D5:XX:XX:XX 2700  # Set to warm white

Note: Devices must be on the same network as your computer.

commands.name: discover
commands.description: Discover LIFX devices on local network

commands.name: list
commands.description: List cached LAN devices from previous discovery

arguments.name: mac-address
arguments.description: MAC address of the light (e.g., D0:73:D5:12:34:56)

arguments.name: kelvin
arguments.description: Color temperature in Kelvin (2500-9000, e.g., 2700 for warm white)

arguments.name: new-name
arguments.description: New name for the light (max 32 characters)

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset configuration to defaults (does not delete API token)
